name: Laromestrocel
summary: Laromestrocel is an allogeneic mesenchymal lineage cell therapy normalized for aging-frailty phase 2b functional evidence.
mechanism_summary: Uses mesenchymal lineage cells to modulate repair and inflammatory signaling, with public evidence focused on older-adult frailty function.

open_questions: Whether the linked evidence generalizes beyond the specific population, model, tissue, or disease context already recorded in the public finding. Linked context: Laromestrocel aging frailty phase 2b trial; Laromestrocel improved 6-minute walk distance in aging frailty.
Whether mechanism-specific effects can be separated from combination, product, delivery, or study-design confounding.
Whether future studies can connect the observed biomarker, functional, or model-organism signal to durable aging-relevant outcomes.
